paper_title: Temporalizing digraphs via linear-size balanced bi-trees
summary: The conjecture that every strong digraph on $n$ vertices has an $O(\log n)$ size forward cover remains open. The source paper (STACS 2024) verifies it for bi-oriented graphs via a centroid-decomposition argument and shows a lower bound (Proposition 4) suggesting the logarithmic bound is tight. No subsequent paper resolving the general case was found in the indexed literature.

internal_refs_verified.note: This paper (Balister, Johnston, Savery, Scott) concerns 1-independent percolation on Z^n — entirely unrelated to forward covers in digraphs. It appears to be a false fuzzy match from the corpus pipeline.

search_queries: forward cover strong digraph log n vertex orderings temporal arxiv 2304.03567
"forward cover" digraph "log n" ordering Bessy Thomassé Viennot temporalizing
notes: No follow-up paper proving or disproving the O(log n) forward cover conjecture was found after 5 web calls. The conjecture is recent (STACS 2024) and likely still open. The paper also proves existence of linear-size balanced bi-trees (the main technical contribution), which is a separate resolved question. The interval nest digraphs paper arXiv:2603.08585 appeared in search results but its abstract did not mention this conjecture.
